summary: High‑energy Alpine Valley show with debut Blow Away & soaring Terrapin
blurb: The June 20, 1988 concert bursts with fresh material – first‑time performances of “Blow Away” and “Victim Or The Crime” – plus a ripping “Terrapin Station” solo. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Phil’s bass warm, and the second set builds into an intense “Other One”. Overall the show ranks as a strong, high‑energy outing worth hearing.
review: Alpine Valley on June 20, 1988 delivered one of the band’s most electric late‑‘80s outings. The first set laid down classic momentum with tight renditions of “Jack Straw”, “Box of Rain” and “Loser”. The second set turned up the heat: Brent debuted the new “Blow Away”, while Jerry vaulted into soaring solos on “Terrapin Station” and “The Other One”, backed by Phil’s resonant bass and a thunderous drum battle that saw Mickey unleash timpani‑like bursts and Bill holding down a steady groove. Highlights also include the debut of “Victim Or The Crime”, a punchy “Good Lovin’”, and an exuberant encore of “U.S. Blues”. While some audience recordings note occasional radio interference, the overall sound captures the raw power of the night, making it a must‑listen for fans who crave high‑energy improvisation and fresh material from this era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings (audience, matrix, nostalgic) express clear positive sentiment about the performance; one soundboard review is mixed but praises vocal quality; the negative review focuses solely on recording mix issues, not musical playing. The consensus points to an energetic, standout show with several praised songs and strong individual performances, justifying a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape captures the high‑energy atmosphere, clear enough sound, and includes the full set with the debut of “Blow Away” and an electrifying second‑set jam.

key_highlights: Debut of Brent’s “Blow Away” and first live performance of “Victim Or The Crime”.
Jerry’s soaring solos on “Terrapin Station”, “Loser”, and the intense “Other One”.
Mickey Hart’s timpani‑like bursts during the drum battle add extra fire.
Overall sound captures the raw, high‑energy vibe despite occasional radio interference.

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring solos on Terrapin Station and Loser, guitar intensity throughout; vocals described as gold and back in top form.
band_performance.Phil: Warm, resonant bass tone that shines especially on Box of Rain, adding a solid foundation to the high‑energy set.
band_performance.Bob: Solid vocal work with occasional lyrical slip (Truckin'), but no major issues noted.
band_performance.Brent: Debuts Blow Away, delivering powerful vocals and rich keyboard textures that drive the new material.
band_performance.Mickey: Timpani‑like bursts and an enthusiastic drum battle that lift the second set to a fever pitch.
band_performance.Billy: Provides steady, supportive drumming that anchors the improvisational peaks.
